hypothesis: If a curated, standardized dataset of Fe K-edge X-ray absorption spectra paired with local atomic structure representations is made available, then machine learning models (including graph neural networks and classical approaches) will be able to learn meaningful bidirectional mappings between iron local coordination environments and XAS spectral features, because the geometric and electronic structural information encoded in the local iron environment is directly reflected in the characteristic features of the Fe K-edge XANES and EXAFS spectra.
